Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst – Global Trade Processors Location: Sydney (Hybrid – 3 days in office)

About the Role

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join our Sydney-based team, working within a global, multidisciplinary Agile environment. This is a key strategic role focused on the rollout of a Global Trade Capture platform across the APAC region, replacing a third-party vendor solution currently used in the region.

You will take a leading role in the APAC migration, acting as the primary point of contact locally and taking on delegated Product Owner responsibilities where required. This role sits at the intersection of business and IT, working closely with developers, operations, commercial teams, and external parties including exchanges, brokers, and clearing houses.

What You'll Be Doing

Translate business needs into clear development requirements and end-to-end solutions

Lead the Global Trade Capture project rollout across APAC, engaging internal and external stakeholders

Document current market landscape, processes, and project progress

Work collaboratively with developers to test and validate solutions

Support root cause analysis on production incidents

Act as first point of contact with Exchanges, Brokers, and Clearing Houses in the region

Provide advice to stakeholders and drive support for change initiatives

Ensure external deadlines are met and all parties are aligned on objectives

Contribute to improvements in performance, quality, and efficiency across processes and systems

What We're Looking For

Relevant Bachelor's or Master's degree

10+ years of experience in Business Analysis

Experience in FIX protocol and trading system components

Background in the listed derivatives and/or equities industry, ideally in a post-trade environment

Proven ability to work on complex IT implementations alongside developers

Strong SQL skills

Experience with GoAnywhere MFT (preferred but not essential)

Comfortable working in an international, multicultural environment across multiple time zones

Able to manage externally imposed deadlines with confidence

Strong analytical skills with the ability to dive into detail when needed

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

Working Arrangements

Sydney office, hybrid working supported

3 days per week in the office required

Flexible working hours to collaborate with global team members across time zones

Why This Role?

This is a high-visibility, strategically important position with genuine ownership across a region. You'll work autonomously within a global team, influence meaningful change, and operate in a fast-paced, professional environment where your expertise will make a real impact.

Must have prior experience in the Teleco industry
3-month CONTRACT with potential extension
Hybrid working arrangement (2-3 days in Melbourne CBD)
Daily rate: $900 (inc SUPER)

ROLE OVERVIEW
We are seeking a highly capable Senior Business Analyst to champion consumer-focused tech initiatives within the telecommunications sector. You will lead an analysis team, partner with project managers across diverse telco programs, and act as the vital link between the PMO, delivery functions, and SMEs. Your mission is to drive continuous improvement, translate complex operational needs into technical requirements, and optimize enterprise systems.

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Lead collaborative workshops to define project scope and evaluate functional workflows using recognized BA frameworks.
Translate operational needs into viable tech designs, producing blueprints, comprehensive documentation, and functional specifications aligned with enterprise architecture.
Act as the primary liaison for internal customers, ensuring proposed solutions meet overarching business goals and commercial boundaries.
Lead and mentor a team of analysts, prioritizing workloads and ensuring rigorous quality control across all deliverables.
Drive the full project lifecycle by executing testing, providing post-implementation support, and championing change management initiatives.

MORE ABOUT YOU

A minimum of 5+ years of BA experience in complex projects, with mandatory prior experience in the Telecommunications industry.

Experience in writing requirements, and user stories etc
Exceptional stakeholder engagement skills, with a proven ability to resolve conflicts, manage client expectations, and facilitate large-scale technical and business workshops.

Comprehensive hands-on experience navigating the full Service Delivery Life Cycle, coupled with strong technical literacy in system design, database concepts, and programming structures.

Proven background in digital transformation initiatives, including expertise in customer journey mapping and wireframing.

Demonstrated success in leading teams and managing deliverables through highly complex landscapes with interrelated project dependencies.

A proactive, autonomous problem-solver with a strong grasp of best-practice administration and governance frameworks.

If this opportunity aligns with your expertise and aspirations, please apply! Please feel free to reach out directly to Muneem Meah at muneem.meah(at)aurec.com, should you have any questions!

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted!

About Zembl

Zembl saves businesses time and improves their bottom line. Our long, strong industry relationships help businesses buy better energy. We have local experts who’ll pick up the phone, have a chat and understand customer needs to take the legwork out of admin. Our simple digital platform and helpful people make admin easy, even pleasurable.

Purpose of the Role

This is a senior individual contributor role in a lean team working on the highest priority problems across the business. You'll drive end-to-end delivery of data and analytics projects across Sales, Operations, Marketing and Finance, acting as the Head of Analytic's right hand on the work that matters most.

You'll flex across analytics, reporting, data quality and ad hoc problem-solving as priorities shift, owning your backlog with a clear focus on moving revenue, conversion and margin metrics.

This role suits someone who's a true data all-rounder, equally at home building a dashboard, writing a pipeline query, digging into a data quality issue, and presenting findings to a senior leader.

About You

You have a commercial mindset and communicate well across all levels of the organisation. You connect analytical work to outcomes, quantify the revenue or margin impact of a finding, and build the business case from the data.

You'll ideally bring:

7+ years across data science, analytics and/or engineering, with breadth across sales, operations and finance

Advanced SQL and Python skills across large, complex datasets and repeatable analytical pipelines

Strong Power BI capability including semantic models, dashboards and visualisations trusted by senior leaders

Hands-on experience with data quality: profiling, testing and resolving issues at source

Practical experience with AI and LLM tools (Claude Code, Cursor, GPT or similar)

Excellent written and verbal communication, from a quick Teams summary to a board-ready insight pack

Experience in propensity modelling, customer retention, segmentation or LTV is highly regarded

Key Responsibilities

End-to-end project delivery

Own analytics projects from problem definition to adoption, with full accountability for quality and impact

Prioritise your backlog around work that moves revenue, conversion and margin

Stakeholder partnership & commercial insight

Translate business questions into actionable analysis for senior leaders across Sales, Marketing and Finance

Drive retention insight through churn, renewal and customer value analysis in partnership with Sales

Own the monthly board reporting cycle with the Head of Analytics

Technical excellence

Write clean, maintainable SQL and Python and build Power BI dashboards and semantic models at scale

Uphold best practice across documentation, version control and QA

Team & data quality

Support colleagues with guidance and peer review

Collaborate with Data Engineering on pipeline and governance improvements

Benefits

Hybrid work from home and in-office approach, plus 2 weeks each year to work from anywhere in the world

2 wellness days per year to rest and recharge

1 volunteer leave day per year to support causes that matter to you

Anniversary leave rewards – earn extra leave the longer you stay (up to 5 days)

Career progression opportunities with homegrown leaders

Up to 16 weeks paid parental leave

Daily perks including 1 free coffee from the onsite barista and a monthly Uber Eats lunch benefit

Senior Data Analyst - 12 Month Contract

📍 Sydney (Hybrid Working)
💰 $1,050 per day + Super

In this role, you will play a critical part in enabling data-driven, evidence-based decision making across a complex technology and business transformation environment. You will work closely with senior stakeholders across business, technology, and delivery functions to ensure key transformation decisions are supported by trusted data, transparent analysis, and actionable insights.

This position is ideal for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, enjoys solving complex data challenges, and can confidently communicate insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Key Responsibilities
Validate and analyse data flows across complex multi-system business and technology environments
Perform data profiling, reconciliation, quality analysis, and issue identification
Translate data defects and inconsistencies into actionable remediation recommendations
Define and document data sourcing logic, controls, assumptions, and governance processes
Design and deliver analytical frameworks, reporting artefacts, and decision-support insights
Produce clear, defensible reporting for executive stakeholders, governance forums, and regulatory audiences
Partner closely with Product Owners, Delivery Leads, Program Managers, Engineers, and SMEs

Support end-to-end analysis of business processes and enterprise data flows
Skills & Experience

Strong experience delivering data analysis within large-scale transformation, regulatory, or technology uplift programs
Advanced SQL skills with the ability to write clean, modular, and performance-optimised queries
Hands-on experience with Python, Excel, and data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau
Experience with source-to-target mapping, data reconciliation, and data profiling

Stakeholder Management & Communication

Delivery & Ways of Working

Familiarity with Git/Bitbucket and collaborative development practices including pull requests and code reviews
Comfortable working within Agile delivery environments alongside cross-functional teams
Mindset & Attributes
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills
High attention to detail and ownership mindset
Proactive, curious, and solutions-focused approach
Comfortable operating in fast-paced environments with evolving priorities
Collaborative team player who contributes positively to delivery culture and knowledge sharing
What's on Offer
Competitive daily rate of $1,050 + Super
12-month contract opportunity on a major enterprise transformation program
Hybrid working environment based in Sydney
Opportunity to work with senior stakeholders across large-scale transformation initiatives
Collaborative and high-performing team culture
Exposure to complex enterprise data and technology landscapes

Job Title: CX Business Analyst – Speech & Text Analytics

Ad Text:
This is a senior officer role with state‑shaping impact. Reporting to the Executive Director (Commercial & Program Governance) within the Rail Infrastructure Delivery Office (RIDO), the Director (Program Performance & Reporting) sets the performance, financial and governance lens for a multi‑billion‑dollar rail investment portfolio critical to Queensland’s future, including delivery ahead of the Brisbane 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games. Operating at enterprise level, you shape how risk, value and benefits are understood by senior executives, central agencies and government decision‑makers. Your work underpins disciplined investment decisions across complex, safety‑critical environments and ensures public funds are managed with authority, transparency and purpose, leaving a lasting imprint on Queensland’s transport network.

Queensland is undertaking one of the most significant rail transformation programs in its history. Reporting to the Executive Director (Commercial & Program Governance), the Director (Program Performance & Reporting) is a pivotal leadership role within the Rail Infrastructure Delivery Office, responsible for the frameworks and insights that give government confidence in the delivery of multibillion‑dollar rail investments.

This role exists to translate scale and complexity into clarity. You determine how performance, financial integrity and procurement outcomes are measured, assured and communicated, enabling informed decision‑making at Executive, Cabinet and whole‑of‑government levels. Your influence extends across major programs of state significance, delivered in a high‑risk, time‑critical environment ahead of the Brisbane 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games.

Operating with significant autonomy, you shape the performance narrative for the rail portfolio, bringing early visibility to emerging risks, trade‑offs and investment consequences that will endure for decades. You sit at the commercial and governance core of the organisation, working in close partnership with the Executive Leadership team to ensure priorities are contested, value is defended and accountability is maintained across complex delivery ecosystems.

As discipline head for program performance and reporting, you set enterprise standards and expectations that reach beyond individual projects. You lead the evolution of analytics, reporting and benefits realisation approaches that support proactive executive action rather than retrospective assurance. Your remit spans statutory reporting, budget processes, audit and assurance, all delivered with the credibility expected of one of the state's most closely scrutinised infrastructure portfolios.

You represent the organisation with senior central agencies and cross‑government forums, shaping how the rail program is positioned with Treasury, national infrastructure bodies and industry. Internally, you are a trusted adviser to senior leaders, providing clear, independent advice on issues carrying material financial, reputational and delivery consequences.

Success in this role is evident in executive confidence, in earlier and better informed decisions, and in infrastructure investments that deliver enduring public value for Queensland communities. This is a career‑defining opportunity to influence outcomes at state and national scale, and to leave a long‑term legacy through disciplined governance of critical public infrastructure.

Applications to remain current for 12 months.

Salary: Attractive package and great co benefits

This position allows for hybrid working arrangements, (WFH & office) in support of teamwork.

Detailed Data Analysis

Material Handling industry

submit your latest CV and Cover letter - explaining why you are a good fit for the role

Company Background

Join a successful, ASX listed digital platform provider that is currently undergoing a significant phase of expansion. This organisation operates at scale, delivering innovative solutions that simplify complex data requirements for millions of users. As a progressive technology business, they provide a supportive environment where individuals can make a genuine impact on award winning products while working alongside industry leaders.

About The Role

This is a unique Technical Business Analyst position designed for someone who enjoys being the face of technology. You will move beyond the back end to actively engage with external stakeholders, ensuring that technical capabilities align perfectly with their operational needs. While you will remain deeply involved in system architecture and data flows, your primary impact will be felt through your ability to navigate client requirements and deliver seamless integration experiences.

Key Responsibilities

Act as the primary technical point of contact for external partners to facilitate smooth platform adoption
Translate sophisticated business requirements into actionable technical specifications for engineering squads
Utilise advanced analytical tools and product usage data to drive informed decision making
Oversee the mapping of data exchanges and provide expert guidance on API integrations and system behaviour
Lead discovery workshops to help clients unlock the full value of the digital ecosystem
Manage and refine the project pipeline using Jira to ensure all delivery milestones are transparent and on track
Ensure all solutions meet rigorous standards for performance, security, and auditability




About You

You are a seasoned analyst who feels just as comfortable in a room of developers as you do presenting to a client. With a solid foundation in technical environments, you have a proven ability to decipher complex systems and explain them in a way that creates value. Proficiency in Jira is essential for managing sophisticated workflows, and experience with data monitoring tools like Splunk is highly regarded for interpreting platform performance.

About Us:

RATP Dev is a subsidiary of the RATP Group, a global leader in high-capacity urban rail and the world’s third largest public transport operator.

We are currently seeking an experienced Senior Data Engineer to join TransitLinX (a John Holland and RATP Dev Joint Venture) based in Melbourne on our Suburban Rail Loop project.

About the Role:

The Senior Data Engineer plays a key role in delivering a strategic Enterprise Data Platform as a core pillar of TransitLinX’s digital transformation. Reporting to the Enterprise Systems & Innovation Manager, this role offers a strong growth opportunity for a hands-on technical professional who wants to contribute to building a Data Lake from the ground up within a structured, international, and high-impact environment. In this role, you will:

Design, develop, and maintain data ingestion and transformation pipelines on AWS and Snowflake

Implement and operate staging, clean, and datamart data layers

Ensure data quality, reliability, and traceability through validation, monitoring, and controls

Provide day-to-day operational support and maintenance of the Data Platform

Analyse business requirements and deliver robust, scalable data solutions

Publish and maintain curated datasets, views, and data models to support multiple business domains

Contribute to the Data Architecture vision and its ongoing evolution

Integrate the Data Lake with broader enterprise systems (asset management, maintenance, ticketing, and external platforms)

Review and challenge technical solutions proposed by system integrators and vendors

Contribute to the progressive implementation of Data Governance foundations, including data quality standards, security principles, and documentation lineage

Work closely with TransitLinX partners and RATP Dev HQ in France to align with corporate standards and best practices

About You:

You are an autonomous, structured, and delivery-focused data professional with a strong architecture mindset. You have the ability to take a step back to assess solutions, while remaining comfortable interacting with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. You are driven by the opportunity to build, structure, and evolve a platform over time within a growing team. You will bring:

6–7 years’ experience in Data Engineering or a similar mid-to-senior technical role

Strong hands-on technical expertise with Python, SQL, and Snowflake

Solid experience designing and operating AWS-based Data platforms (incorporating tools like Amazon MWAA/Apache Airflow and dbt Core)

Deep understanding of Data Lake architectures, ELT/ETL orchestration, governance, and enterprise data platforms

The ability to work on broader architecture and systems integration topics, rather than just data pipelines

Fluency in English (mandatory as the day-to-day working language)

Knowledge of Asset Management systems (e.g., Maximo, maintenance, lifecycle management) (desirable)

Experience working within the transport, infrastructure, or industrial sectors (desirable)

Familiarity with construction and/or operations data (desirable)

Relevant tertiary qualifications and/or equivalent knowledge gained through a combination of education, training and/or experience in a similar role
5+ years professional experience working in process analysis on high visibility and complex programs
Proven experience defining, embedding and improving business processes
Proven experience in Microsoft Dynamics 365 implementations

Proficiency with process modelling tools such as Visio or other business process management software
Excellent analytical skills with ability to identify and advise on process improvement opportunities

Demonstrated understanding of Agile methodologies having worked in Agile environments.

Proven experience leading knowledge management functions in complex organisations
Strong strategic thinking and ability to influence and collaborate across all levels
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Deep understanding of collaborative and information platforms and their potential
Demonstrated ability to lead capability development and cultural change initiatives
Analytical mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

The Opportunity
Our client is a powerhouse in the Australian industrial services sector, supporting critical industries from mining to agriculture with a national footprint of over 1,000 employees. They are currently executing a high-stakes, multi-year Finance Transformation Program designed to modernize their systems and create an insight-led finance model.
  
We are looking for a Finance Technology Specialist who can act as the vital "translator" between the Finance, Technology, and PMO departments. This is not a role for those who prefer to stay on the sidelines; you will be the system-side authority driving the design and delivery of their ERP roadmap.
  
Key Responsibilities
Serve as the Finance Design Authority for all ERP and technology implementations.
  
Drive hands-on system configuration and set-up to ensure technology meets complex accounting requirements.
  
Lead the definition of functional specifications, data structures, and reporting hierarchies.
  
Own the Finance-side of project delivery, including User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and data migration validation.
  
Identify and implement design-led improvements to reduce manual rework and strengthen internal controls.
  
Influence and align stakeholders across more than 50 locations to adopt new ways of working.
  
The Ideal Candidate

The successful candidate will be a rare blend: someone with a "Big Picture" design mindset who isn't afraid to get into the weeds of configuration.

  ERP Expertise (Mandatory): Extensive experience with ERP implementations is required, with a heavy preference for candidates with hands-on Infor LN experience.
    
  Financial Foundation: You possess a strong accounting background and a deep understanding of financial reporting, controls, and core modules like GL, AP, AR, and Fixed Assets.
  
  Academic Background: You hold a Bachelor’s degree in Commerce or Technology.
    
  Transformation Savvy: Experience in complex, multi-entity, or acquisitive environments where you have driven operating model change is highly desirable.  
  
  Tech Stack: Proficiency in data and reporting tools such as Power BI, SQL, or advanced Excel is a distinct advantage.
Why Apply?

If you are looking for a role where you can apply best-practice thinking to a rapidly growing organization, this is it. You will have the authority to challenge legacy practices and play a central role in building a modern, integrated industrial business.
  
  
To apply or learn more about this transformation-focused role, please submit your CV for a confidential discussion.

Ad Text:
Shape Your World

At Alcoa, you will become an essential part of our purpose: to turn raw potential into real progress. The way we see it, every Alcoan is a work-shaper, team-shaper, idea-shaper & world-shaper.

As a leader within Alcoa, you can help fulfill our purpose and realise our vision to build a legacy of excellence for future generations. Be part of the team that is helping shape a better workplace with the flexibility and equal opportunities that help everyone thrive. You have the power to shape things and individuals to make them better.

About the Role

Are you a strategic thinker with a passion for turning data into powerful business insights? Join Alcoa’s WA Mining team as a Principal ABS – Performance Insights and play a pivotal role in shaping data-driven decision making across our WA Mining Operations operations. This role is offered on a Monday to Friday roster based at our Mining Hub in Pinjarra.

This is a key leadership position where you will move beyond reporting to deliver predictive insights, performance optimisation and strategic direction, helping drive operational excellence and long-term business success

As the Principal ABS – Performance Insights, you will:

Define and execute the analytics roadmap aligned to business strategy and priorities.

Lead analysis of complex datasets to identify trends, risks and opportunities.

Translate data into clear, actionable insights that drive performance improvement.

Partner with senior leaders as a trusted advisor, influencing decision making.

Establish best practice in data governance, reporting and analytics processes.

Lead, coach and mentor others, building capability and a culture of continuous improvement.

You will play a critical role in transforming raw data into meaningful narratives that guide strategic planning, operational efficiency and future growth

What’s on offer

Flexible hybrid working options available.

18 weeks Parental leave for primary caregivers.

One additional paid Alcoa Flexi Day.

Alcoa Live Well program offering travel, lifestyle, health and wellbeing discounts.

Generous leave entitlements including a leisure a day off every 4-week period.

Salary packaging for a novated car lease, employee share plan and superannuation options.

What you can bring to the role

Tertiary qualification in Engineering, Analytics, Business Intelligence or similar, coupled with significant experience in a data-driven or analytics-focused role.

Proven experience in a senior or leadership capacity.

Strong technical capability in SQL, Python or R, and BI tools such as Power BI or Tableau.

Exceptional communication, influencing and stakeholder engagement skills.

Experience within mining or heavy industry (highly desirable).

Additional information

Interviews may progress prior to the closing date, although all applications will be considered.

You will only be contacted if you are shortlisted for an interview, this process can take up to four weeks from the closing date.

Be part of shaping our organisation and join us in building a legacy of excellence for future generations.

#AlcoaAUS

#LI-SV1  

About the Location

Huntly is one of our two bauxite mines in Western Australia. Established in 1976 and located east of North Dandalup in WA, it is the world’s second largest bauxite mine, supplying ore to Alcoa’s Pinjarra alumina refinery. Bauxite ore from Alcoa’s Huntly and Willowdale mines helps to produce almost half of Australia’s alumina and approximately 19 per cent of Australia’s aluminium. Our workplace is an inclusive and respectful environment, where we embrace change, new ideas and equal opportunity to succeed. 

We are values led, vision driven and united by our purpose of transforming raw potential into real progress.  Our commitments to Inclusion, Diversity & Equity include providing trusting workplaces that are safe, respectful and inclusive of all individuals, free from discrimination, bullying and harassment and that our workplaces reflect the diversity of the communities in which we operate.    

This is a place where you are empowered to do your best work, be your authentic self, and feel a true sense of belonging. Come join us and shape your career!  

Your work. Your world. Shape them for the better.

About the business/role/team

As Senior Manager, Information and Data Governance, you will report to the Deputy Head of Knowledge Management and lead a team of specialists across Information and Data Governance, Compliance and Architecture teams, where you will work closely with senior leaders in Risk, Technology, and Business Units to embed governance principles and enable data-driven decision-making.

This team sits within the Bank's Knowledge Management Department, which is central to enterprise information strategy and compliance.

About the role


Manage enterprise Information, Data, and AI governance frameworks, aligned with Commonwealth legislative requirements and Bank policy.
Manage the Bank's information classification framework, ensuring sensitive information is appropriately classified, handled, and protected, and maintain alignment with the Protective Security Policy Framework (PSPF), Australian Government Information Security Manual (ISM), and internal security standards.
Ensure compliance with recordkeeping obligations, including the Archives Act and relevant standards for digital and physical records.
Ensure that data models, standards, and platforms align with governance, security, and compliance requirements.
Partner with technology and business leaders to ensure governance supports innovation, analytics, and AI adoption while mitigating risk.
Champion cultural change by promoting information and data stewardship, security awareness, and information integrity across the organisation.


About you


Deep expertise in information and data governance, including regulatory compliance, risk management, and emerging AI governance practices.
Strong knowledge of Commonwealth information security and recordkeeping frameworks, including PSPF, ISM, and Archives Act requirements.
Proven leadership experience in complex organisations, ideally in financial services or government.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at all levels and drive enterprise-wide adoption of governance principles.
Familiarity with data platforms, metadata management, records management and governance technologies.

About Lactalis-Mainland Dairy


Mainland Dairy, the business formerly known as Fonterra’s global Consumer and associated businesses, is now part of the Lactalis Group and known as Lactalis-Mainland Dairy. 


Lactalis is a French family-owned business founded in Laval in 1933. Today, it is the world’s leading dairy group, present in 50 countries employing around 85,500 people. It is home to some of the world’s best known global and international dairy brands, including Président, Galbani and Parmalat, and local brands including Pauls, OAK, and Cracker Barrel in Australia.  


Lactalis-Mainland Dairy is a team of more than 4,300 employees, operating 16 manufacturing sites across Oceania, Southeast Asia, South Asia and the Middle East. We make well-loved brands including Perfect Italiano, Mainland, and Western Star. 


Our passionate and talented team is committed to nurturing the future, unlocking new opportunities, and fostering innovation in nutrition and food.  


The Opportunity


Reporting to the Finance System Compliance Specialist, the Finance System Reporting Lead is responsible for the ownership, stability and integrity of finance systems and system driven reporting across the organisation.


This role plays a pivotal part in ensuring accurate, timely and reliable financial outputs across management reporting, allocations, forecasting and consolidation. Acting as a key link between Finance, IT and business stakeholders, you will support month end and planning cycles while enabling effective decision making through high quality system reporting and insights.


A core element of the role involves close collaboration with offshore Hub teams, Group and Regional FP&A, ensuring accuracy of Hub prepared reports and alignment during ongoing management reporting system transition.


In this role, you will:


Own and administer core finance systems (SAP ECC/S/4, Central Finance and planning/forecasting tools), ensuring stability, accuracy and strong data governance 
Manage allocation, settlement and close cycle processes (actuals and forecast) to support management reporting and decision making 
Partner closely with Finance, FP&A, IT and offshore Hub teams to resolve system issues, support transitions and ensure high quality reporting outputs 
Deliver monthly management reporting, insights and analysis on revenue, cost and margin performance 
Support budgeting, forecasting, audit requirements and continuous improvement across finance systems and reporting processes


About You


You are a finance system–focused professional who enjoys working at the intersection of systems, data and commercial insight. You bring a collaborative mindset, strong technical capability and the confidence to work with stakeholders across Finance, IT and the wider business.


To succeed in this role, you’ll ideally bring:


A degree in Accounting, Finance or a related discipline, with CA/CPA (or equivalent) preferred 
Strong experience supporting management reporting, allocations, forecasting and close processes within complex finance systems 
Hands on knowledge of SAP ECC/S/4, Central Finance and planning or forecasting tools (FICO/COPA experience highly regarded) 
Well developed analytical and problem solving skills, with the ability to translate financial data into clear insights 
Strong communication skills and the ability to operate effectively in a matrixed, multinational environment


What We Offer


Competitive pay and benefits
Career development and internal opportunities across a global business
A team focused workplace in a fantastic culture where your work has real impact
A strong commitment to quality, innovation and sustainability
Flexible work arrangements where possible


Based at our Richmond office, this role offers excellent career development and internal mobility pathways as well as flexible ways of working and a hybrid structure, that allows you the balance of in-office collaboration and working from home to suit your lifestyle. 


Please note that applicants must have full working rights for Australia to be considered.
We are politely declining recruitment agency support and approaches for this role.

Company: Department of Transport and Main Roads
Ad Text:
Are you a strategic leader with a passion for data, reporting and shaping major infrastructure programs? In the role of Manager (Reporting), you will lead the delivery of high quality reporting and analysis for the Queensland Transport and Roads Investment Program (QTRIP), supporting key state and federal budget processes, Estimates, and whole of government performance requirements. You'll provide expert advice to senior leaders, drive improvements in reporting frameworks and data quality, and deliver clear, insightful analysis that informs decision making.
This role offers the opportunity to influence how the department monitors and communicates program performance, while building capability, leading a high performing team, and contributing to outcomes that benefit communities across Queensland.

In the role of Manager (Reporting) you will lead and provide direction on the reporting and analysis for the department's annual Queensland Transport and Roads Investment Program (Q TRIP) publication, including preparing and providing information for state and federal budgetary processes, Estimates Committee Hearings, and whole-of-government program performance reporting requirements. You will provide high level strategic advice, expertise and leadership in relation to program reporting for the department.

Some of the key responsibilities for this role include:

Lead and provide direction for the preparation and delivery of the QTRIP and State Budget documentation, including Budget Paper 3 — Capital Statement (BP3), Regional Delivery Plans (RDPS) and Service Delivery Statement (SDS), as well as the department's Annual Report.
Lead and manage the preparation of insightful program performance analysis for delivery areas and senior executives and respond to internal and external reporting needs, including the Transport Infrastructure Portfolio performance and the whole-of-government Capital Program performance. This includes developing reporting and dashboard processes and templates, procedures and guidelines, extracting relevant financial and non-financial data, and conducting data verification activities.

We recognise and celebrate that everyone is unique and seek the applicant best suited to the role. We will assess your experience, knowledge and acquired skills, as well as your potential for development and your personal qualities. To thrive in this role, you will need to demonstrate the following:

Mandatory qualifications and conditions

Due to this role having access to sensitive data, a criminal history check will beundertaken prior to appointment to the role.

Requirements of the role

Proven experience in interpreting and analysing large, complex data sets to prepare spreadsheets, dashboards, reports and presentations in an easy-to-read format. Including a high degree of proficiency in data analysis tools and software is essential, particularly Microsoft Excel.
Demonstrated experience in driving continuous improvement and providing innovative solutions in systems and processes related to reporting.
Highly developed stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to work collaboratively with internal stakeholders across the department and external partners, including Queensland Treasury and the Department of State Development, Infrastructure and Planning.

Some of the additional benefits this opportunity offers include:

Work/life balance, including 'flex time' and flexible work arrangements and a working from home option.
City location close to active and public transport, with just over a 10 minute walk from Central Station.
Work with a supportive team, with a high level of training and development invested in each staff member
Generous leave and superannuation 

For more information on the full list of responsibilities, requirements of the role and how to apply for this exciting opportunity, please read the Role Description and Applicant Guide.
